validation_summary.evidence: python -m py_compile exits 0 (~0.1s); python -m unittest skills.development-harness.scripts.tests.integration.test_self_test_run -v passes 1/1 (2.1s); python -m unittest discover skills/development-harness/scripts/tests passes 345/346 with 1 OS-specific skip (40.6s, up from 345 -- 1 new case for unit_056 + trace.log artifact)

checkpoint.summary: unit_056 complete. Programmatic self-test run against the Tasklet fixture: integration test_self_test_run.py spawns a temp git repo, reads fixtures/self-test/phase-graph.json + config.json, drives the full compute_parallel_batch -> dispatch_batch -> fake-sub-agent-commits -> merge_batch pipeline for multiple turns until the fixture reaches a stable terminal state. All three seeded conditions fired exactly as designed: Turn 1 batch [a1, a2] + exclusion 'unit_a3 path_overlap_with:unit_a2' + both merged; Turn 2 a3 alone as batch-of-1 + clean merge; Turn 3 full batch [b1, b2, b3] with outcome 'partial' (b1+b3 merged, b2 rejected for scope_violation on src/seeds/users.json). Final: 5 units completed + 1 pending (b2), only unit_b2's worktree surviving (scope-violation path preserves it per merge_batch.py). Batch sizes observed: [2, 1, 3] -- max 3 satisfies the 'batch >= 2' acceptance. Trace log committed to fixtures/self-test/trace.log as evidence for units 057 + 058. Two bugs caught during implementation: FIXTURE_DIR path computation had a double 'scripts/' + orphan check rglob saw the batch-parent dir in addition to leaf dirs -- both fixed. Suite 345 -> 346.
checkpoint.next_action: Complete unit_057 (PHASE_013): produce a data table comparing wall-clock with parallelism enabled vs a sequential baseline; verify no orphaned worktrees/branches after completion. The parallel wall-clock is already captured in trace.log from unit_056 (3 turns: ~2s total in the test). For the sequential baseline, run the same fixture with execution_mode.parallelism.enabled=false (forces batch-of-1 in-tree fast path throughout) -- would need 6 turns one unit at a time. Compare wall-clock between the two. The orphan check is already passing in unit_056's test (only b2's worktree survives by design). Write the data table as a short markdown snippet that will be embedded in POST-MORTEM.md (unit_058). Validation: comparison data committed as part of the fixture; grep verifies the table includes both parallel + sequential rows + a wall-clock ratio.
